title: Bitcoin Mining Firm Riot Platforms Records $167 Million Revenue In Q1 2026: Report
text: Riot Platforms reported $167.2 million in total Q1 2026 revenue, with Bitcoin mining revenue of $111.9 million and new data center segment revenue of $33.2 million. The company achieved significant growth in its data center operations, marking a definitive inflection point toward AI infrastructure. Cost to mine bitcoin was $44,629 per bitcoin excluding depreciation in Q1 2026, down from approximately $43,808 in Q1 2025. Power curtailment credits increased 169% year-over-year, demonstrating the value of Riot's power portfolio management strategy. The company mined 1,473 bitcoin in the quarter and maintained 15,679 bitcoin in holdings. Cash position remained strong at $282.5 million including restricted cash. The AMD lease expansion to 50 MW total capacity represents a major catalyst for future revenue growth, with the partnership generating approximately $636 million in projected revenue over the primary 10-year contract period. Engineering revenue of $22.2 million showed growth from $13.9 million in the prior year period.
